evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
FAQPage schema has 9 Questions but page shows ~15 FAQ pairs

No TL;DR block (expected

or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')
No visible 'Last updated' / 'Last reviewed' / 'Next review' stamp on page (schema dateModified alone isn't enough for users or AI)
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Human voice vs committee 4/10 — Has flashes of voice ('who's on aux') but mostly reads like SEO template output with SERP-style headings.

suggestedPatch.target: Oahu doesn’t lack options, it’s about shrinking the overload.
suggestedPatch.before: Oahu doesn’t lack options, it’s about shrinking the overload.
suggestedPatch.after: Oahu has too much to do, not too little—the job is cutting the list before your group chat explodes.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Human voice vs committee" scored 4/10: Has flashes of voice ('who's on aux') but mostly reads like SEO template output with SERP-style headings.

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — Some opinions exist (Duke's is touristy but fine, Pig and the Lady non-negotiable) but most claims are hedged generalities.

suggestedPatch.target: Oahu’s main beaches and attractions are set up for groups and families.
suggestedPatch.before: Oahu’s main beaches and attractions are set up for groups and families.
suggestedPatch.after: Oahu's main beaches work for groups; skip Sandy Beach and Makapu'u tide pools unless everyone's a strong swimmer.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 6/10: Some opinions exist (Duke's is touristy but fine, Pig and the Lady non-negotiable) but most claims are hedged generalities.

evidence: Unexpected phrasings 6/10 — A few good lines ('who's on aux', 'survive the group chat') but padded with predictable phrasing elsewhere.

suggestedPatch.target: Not all Oahu itineraries survive the group chat.
suggestedPatch.before: Not all Oahu itineraries survive the group chat.
suggestedPatch.after: Keep the opener but follow with something less predictable than 'lost in polls and side threads.'
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 6/10: A few good lines ('who's on aux', 'survive the group chat') but padded with predictable phrasing elsewhere.

evidence: Point of view vs both-sides 6/10 — Takes stances in places but FAQ section drifts into neutral survey mode.

suggestedPatch.target: Waikiki offers walkable beaches and (relatively) easy meals; Ala Moana gives bigger rentals and park access. Kailua is quiet and best for groups with a car.
suggestedPatch.before: Waikiki offers walkable beaches and (relatively) easy meals; Ala Moana gives bigger rentals and park access. Kailua is quiet and best for groups with a car.
suggestedPatch.after: Stay in Ala Moana. Waikiki is overpriced for what you get, and Kailua strands you without a car.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view vs both-sides" scored 6/10: Takes stances in places but FAQ section drifts into neutral survey mode.

evidence: Quotable sentences 5/10 — 'Who's on aux' is quotable; most sentences are functional rather than shareable.

suggestedPatch.target: Beach, food, activity: one per day keeps the trip on track, and nobody burnt out.
suggestedPatch.before: Beach, food, activity: one per day keeps the trip on track, and nobody burnt out.
suggestedPatch.after: One beach, one meal worth the detour, one thing to brag about—that's the daily cap before a group fractures.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotable sentences" scored 5/10: 'Who's on aux' is quotable; most sentences are functional rather than shareable.

evidence: specificity 6/10 — The budget section introduces a cost table but the surrounding prose retreats into abstractions — 'costs can spiral,' 'split smart,' 'saving hundreds.' The table itself is specific, but the framing copy fails to anchor the advice in a concrete scenario or named comparison. The sentence flagged floats completely free of any number, property name, or dollar figure, undermining the otherwise data-rich post.

suggestedPatch.target: Costs can spiral fast if you don't split smart.
suggestedPatch.before: Costs can spiral fast if you don't split smart.
suggestedPatch.after: A 4-person group splitting a 2-bedroom Ala Moana vacation rental (typically $350–$500/night) pays roughly $630–$875 each for 7 nights — versus $1,400+ per person at a comparable Waikiki hotel like the Alohilani.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 6/10: The budget section introduces a cost table but the surrounding prose retreats into abstractions — 'costs can spiral,' 'split smart,' 'saving hundreds.' The table itself is specific, but the framing copy fails to anchor the advice in a concrete scenario or named comparison. The sentence flagged floats completely free of any number, property name, or dollar figure, undermining the otherwise data-rich post.
